name: Fever
plain_description: A fever means the body temperature is higher than usual. It is often a sign that the body is reacting to an infection or another illness.
suggested_action: Rest, drink fluids, and monitor the temperature and any new symptoms. Seek medical advice if the fever is very high, lasts, or is linked with other warning signs.
warning_signs: fever that returns and gets worse

name: Cough
plain_description: A cough is a reflex that helps clear the throat and airways. It can happen with short-term infections or longer-term conditions.
suggested_action: Drink water, rest, and watch for how long the cough lasts. Short-term coughs often improve, but a long-lasting or worsening cough should be checked.

name: Sore Throat
plain_description: A sore throat is pain, discomfort, or scratchiness in the throat. It is commonly linked to viral infections but can have other causes.
suggested_action: Drink fluids and consider soothing measures such as lozenges or warm salt-water gargles. If it is severe or does not improve, medical review may be needed.

name: Headache
plain_description: Headache is one of the most common types of pain. Many headaches are not serious, but some can be a warning sign of an urgent problem.
suggested_action: Rest, hydrate, and note when the headache started and whether it is new, sudden, or linked with other symptoms.

name: Shortness of Breath
plain_description: Shortness of breath means it feels hard or uncomfortable to get enough air. It can happen for mild reasons, but it can also signal a serious heart or lung problem.
suggested_action: If symptoms are mild and brief, monitor closely. If breathing is difficult, worsening, or linked with chest pain, urgent medical care is needed.

name: Chest Pain
plain_description: Chest pain can come from the heart, lungs, digestive tract, muscles, or other tissues. Some causes are mild, but some need immediate care.
suggested_action: Chest pain should be taken seriously, especially if it is new, persistent, crushing, or linked with other urgent symptoms.
warning_signs: pain that does not go away

name: Abdominal Pain
plain_description: Abdominal pain is pain anywhere between the chest and groin. It can come from many organs, so the cause is not always obvious from the pain alone.
suggested_action: Monitor where the pain is, how severe it is, and what other symptoms happen with it. Sudden sharp pain or pain with bleeding needs urgent care.

name: Nausea and Vomiting
plain_description: Nausea is the feeling that you may vomit, and vomiting is when stomach contents come up through the mouth. They are common symptoms with many possible causes.
suggested_action: Take small amounts of clear fluids to avoid dehydration and avoid foods or smells that worsen symptoms.

name: Diarrhea
plain_description: Diarrhea means loose or watery stools. Short-term diarrhea is common, but it can become dangerous when it causes dehydration or bleeding.
suggested_action: Replace lost fluids and monitor for dehydration. Persistent diarrhea or diarrhea with fever, blood, or severe pain should be checked.

name: Fatigue
plain_description: Fatigue is more than just being sleepy. It is a sense of low energy that can affect daily life and may come from lifestyle factors or illness.
suggested_action: Review sleep, stress, activity, and other symptoms. Fatigue lasting for weeks should be medically reviewed.

name: Rash
plain_description: A rash is an irritated or swollen area of skin. It may be itchy, red, painful, blistered, or patchy depending on the cause.
suggested_action: Note when the rash started, whether it is spreading, and whether there are trigger exposures such as new medicines or chemicals.

name: Dizziness
plain_description: Dizziness may mean feeling faint, woozy, or off balance. Some people mean vertigo, where it feels as if they or the room are spinning.
suggested_action: Sit or lie down safely, hydrate if appropriate, and monitor whether symptoms are brief or keep returning.

name: Runny or Stuffy Nose
plain_description: A runny or stuffy nose is often caused by inflammation in the nasal lining. It commonly happens with colds, flu, sinus problems, or allergies.
suggested_action: Monitor for duration, facial swelling, and throat symptoms. Many mild cases improve on their own within about a week.

source.name: MedlinePlus Medical Encyclopedia - Stuffy or Runny Nose

name: Back Pain
plain_description: Back pain is very common and may feel dull, stiff, or sharp. Many cases improve with time, but severe or injury-related pain should be assessed.
suggested_action: Avoid long bed rest, note whether there was an injury, and watch whether pain improves over a few days.
warning_signs: pain not improving after several days

name: Allergic Reaction
plain_description: An allergic reaction can range from mild itching or rash to a severe reaction called anaphylaxis, which can become life-threatening quickly.
suggested_action: Watch closely for breathing difficulty, facial swelling, or worsening symptoms after exposure to a likely allergen.
